entities.name: MARUCA VITRATA
entities.description: Legume pod borer, a major pest constraining the production and productivity of grain legumes in the tropics

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: Agricultural crops that are hosts to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic region where grain legume production is significantly affected by Maruca vitrata

entities.name: SCREENING FOR RESISTANCE
entities.description: The process of evaluating plant genotypes for their ability to resist Maruca vitrata

entities.name: NATURAL INFESTATION
entities.description: A screening method using naturally occurring pest populations to identify resistance

entities.name: MULTI-CHOICE TESTS
entities.description: A screening method where pests can choose between different host plant genotypes

entities.name: NO-CHOICE TESTS
entities.description: A screening method where pests are forced to feed on a specific host plant genotype

entities.name: GREENHOUSE/LABORATORY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled environments used to conduct multi- and no-choice resistance tests

entities.name: GENOTYPIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherent genetic ability of a plant to resist attack by Maruca vitrata

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A grain legume for which genotypic resistance to Maruca vitrata has been documented

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A grain legume for which information on resistance to Maruca vitrata is limited

entities.name: STEM THICKNESS
entities.description: A physical plant attribute associated with resistance to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: POD WALL THICKNESS
entities.description: A physical plant attribute associated with resistance to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: TRICHOMES
entities.description: Hair-like appendages on plant surfaces associated with resistance to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: PODDING HABIT
entities.description: The characteristic way a plant produces pods, associated with resistance to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: NATURAL ENEMIES
entities.description: Organisms that prey upon or parasitize Maruca vitrata

entities.name: CULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural management strategies used to reduce pest damage

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: A cultural practice of growing two or more crops together to reduce Maruca vitrata damage in cowpea

entities.name: WEEDING
entities.description: A cultural practice of removing weeds to reduce Maruca vitrata damage in cowpea

entities.name: TIME OF PLANTING
entities.description: A cultural practice involving the scheduling of planting to reduce Maruca vitrata damage in cowpea

entities.name: PLANTING DENSITY
entities.description: A cultural practice involving the spacing of plants to reduce Maruca vitrata damage in cowpea

entities.name: INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used for the control of Maruca vitrata

entities.name: INSECT-PLANT-ENVIRONMENT INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The complex relationship between the pest, the host plant, and the surrounding environment

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to identify and evaluate resistance in host plants

entities.name: MECHANISMS OF RESISTANCE
entities.description: The biological and physical processes that enable a plant to resist a pest

entities.name: DIVERSITY OF RESISTANCE
entities.description: The variation in resistance traits across different plant genotypes

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of introducing foreign genetic material into host plants to confer resistance

entities.name: BT GENES
entities.description: Genes from Bacillus thuringiensis used in genetic transformation to provide pest resistance

entities.name: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A holistic approach to pest control combining biological, cultural, and chemical tools

entities.name: AGRO-ECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Diverse agricultural environments where integrated pest management is applied
